actions.prompt:
OCR the screenshot only, whatever text is present reproduce it as output and nothing else.

IF the user provides text context with the image as input, then he typically wants you to insert what you OCR appropriately where it would fit in that text input. So for instance if he had context of Comparison Exams: None, and OCRD a list of few prior exams, then change that to list the dates appropriately.
actions.label: OCR

actions.prompt: Describe exactly what is seen in the provided image as it would be described in a radiology report. Focus only on the depicted imaging findings or normal appearance. Use concise radiology-style language and objective descriptive terminology. If the user provides guidance, a template, or asks for a specific structure, follow that. Otherwise, sound exactly like a real radiology report description. Do not add markdown, bullet points, bolding, headings, or conversational commentary. Do not begin any sentence with the word The or the phrase There is.
actions.label: DESCRIBE

actions.prompt:
User will upload multiple images, your job is to process all of them (with grounding of an elite specialist radiology master interpreter), but then individually crop and cut then and zoom and preocess them individually appropriately, so you get a full picture

Guardrailes are not to proceed until you confirm u have images, you've assessed normal, and you've assessed abnormal. Use chain of thought and sequential thinking. Do all of this internally.

Then you fill in the report according to what you found

Use this template 

STUDY: XR Spine Thoracic 3 View 72072.

REASON FOR EXAM: Pain.

COMPARISON: None.

FINDINGS/IMPRESSION:
Normal thoracic spinal alignment.

Relative intact vertebral body statures, allowing for radiographic limitation and osseous overlap.

No aggressive degenerative changes appreciated.

-----

Clinical correlation recommended; consider more advanced imaging if symptoms persist.

If dynamic images were not provided then do not discuss dynamic exam. 

you only have to include the FINDINGS/IMPRESSION part not the other sections (study, reason for exam etc)

BUT Make the wording much more skilled efficient no BS elite radiologist < Also you must adjust the wording to accound for any findings you see in the images. Image findings take precidence.
actions.label: SPINE

actions.prompt:
Interpret (from a reporting radiologist perpective) the screenshot only, reproduce radiology description as output and nothing else.

User will also provide the template for what he wants you to do, inject the findings from what you ocr (or from the radiology images provided and you interpret) into the appropriate sections into the template the user provides.

If user provides template, fill in the details. Just change what is pertinant from the OCR.

Do not begin sentences with "The" or "There is".
actions.label: INJECT

actions.prompt: Make sure you describe what the image shows, make sure you are grounded in experise at the level of Atlas of Normal Roentgen Variants That May Simulate Diseas by Keats. True radiology expertise.
actions.label: Variants

actions.prompt:
Describe as if it is a radiology report the findings of two images and compare across time. The current (most recent) image will be marked with a cicrle. The prior (previous scan image) will have none.
Don't begin sentences with "The" or "There is"

Dont include description of the circle (its just for marking for you). Use language as if its a expert radiologist describing it in a report body.

User may include descriptions in the black (not in the imaging part) to help guide your interpretation and grounding (anatomically and such)

Round to nearest 10th.
actions.label: COMPARE

actions.prompt: Describe exactly what anatomy is being pinpointed by arrow. Use all resources available (including external) to make sure you are accurate and precise. Also reprocess the image by zooming in and utilizing multiple processing rounds. Dont use special characters as output such as **
actions.label: Anatomy

actions.prompt:
Interpret (from a reporting radiologist perpective) the screenshot only, reproduce radiology description as output and nothing else.

Only describe the abnormal findings, not the normal ones. Use radiology language. Make sure you comment on evereything abnormal that a experiened radiologist would. 

Do not begin sentences with "The" or "There is".
actions.label: LIST
